# Queueing Theory

> References: [[03-resource/Investigación de Operaciones - Teoría de Colas\|Investigación de Operaciones - Teoría de Colas]]
> Sources: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Queueing_theory

A Queueing Theory consist of [[Mathematica\|Mathematical]] formulas and relationships that can be used to determine the operating characteristics for a waiting line. 
## Queue Discipline
The [[Queue\|Queue]] discipline for the waiting line systems are used to define the manner in which the waiting units are arranged. This can be:
- [[First In First Out\|First In First Out]]
- [[Last In First Out\|Last In First Out]]
- [[Priority Queue\|Priority Queue]]
## Queue Configuration
### Stationary State
For some models to work, the queue must be in a stationary state, meaning that the probability of $n$ clients in a period of time states constant. 
### Single Server
> [!note] 
> También conocido como 1 Cola / 1 Servidor 
> Puede denotarse mediante X/X/1 en [[01-atomic/unsolved/Kendall's Notation\|Kendall's Notation]], donde X depende del modelo

### Multiple Servers
> [!note] 
> También conocido como 1 Cola / Multiples Servidores
> Puede denotarse mediante X/X/k en [[01-atomic/unsolved/Kendall's Notation\|Kendall's Notation]], donde hay $k$ servidores y X depende del modelo.

### Tandem Queues
Tandem queues are a type of queue where customers must pass between multiple queues. The output of one queue goes to the next.

### Priority queues

## Parameters

> [!warning] Sobre los "Rate" o Tiempos
> En [[02-moc/INF293 - Investigación de Operaciones\|IO]] es común de que los tiempos se den de la forma "un número $\lambda$ de personas en una razón de tiempo $t$". Este dato lo denote como $\lambda_t$, que corresponde a $\lambda \times t$. Tener ojo con que el dato para la probabilidad del tiempo en un servicio o la probabilidad de que lleguen $x$ clientes.
### Arrival Rate
> [!definition] Arrival Rate
> #definition The arrival rate ($\lambda$) is defined as the mean number of arrivals per period

> [!formula] Mean time between Arrivals
> #formula $$\text{Mean time between Arrivals} = \frac{1}{\lambda}$$

The arrival rate can be model as an [[01-atomic/math/Poisson Probability Distribution\|Poisson Probability Distribution]] 
- The clients arrive in order (uno a uno)
- If the arrivals are **stationary** ([[Probability\|Probability]] between a time $t$ and $t+h$ just depends of the size of $h$)
- If the arrivals are **Independent** (One client doesn't change the probability of other)

Then the arrival rate is described as a [[Random Variable\|Random Variable]] $X$. 

> [!formula] Probability of $x$ arrivals in a time period
> #formula$$P(X = x) = P(x) = \frac{(\lambda t)^{x}e^{-\lambda t}}{x!}$$
> where
> - $x$ = number of arrivals in time period
> - $\lambda$ = mean number of arrivals (Arrival Rate)

> [!note] Tiempo en la formula 
> Notar que en las formulas para la teoria de colas, generalmente para el ratio de 
### Service Rate

The service time is the time a customer spends at the service facility once the service started

> [!definition] Service Rate
> #definition The service rate ($\mu$) is the mean number of units that can be served per period

A common [[Probability Distribution\|Probability Distribution]] used for the service rate is the [[01-atomic/math/Exponential Probability Distribution\|Exponential Probability Distribution]].

> [!formula] Mean time between Services
> #formula $$\text{Mean time between Services} = \frac{1}{\mu}$$

> [!warning]
> En el certamen de [[02-moc/INF293 - Investigación de Operaciones\|IO]] es común que se entregue el tiempo medio entre servicios (correspondiente a $\frac{1}{\mu}$) en vez del $\mu$. Tener cuidado.

> [!formula] Probability that the service time will be less than or equal to time $t$
> #formula $$P(\text{service time} \leq t) = 1 - e^{\mu t}$$
> where
> - $t$ = Time
> - $\mu$ = Service rate

### Utilization Rate

> [!definition] Utilization Rate
> #definition Utilization Rate $(\rho)$ defined as the ration between the [[01-atomic/unsolved/Queueing Theory#Arrival Rate\|#Arrival Rate]] and the [[01-atomic/unsolved/Queueing Theory#Service Rate\|#Service Rate]].
> #formula $$\rho = \frac{\lambda}{\mu}$$
## Operating Characteristics

There are seven characteristics used to compute the steady-state characteristics.

1. [[Probability\|Probability]] that no units are in the system $(P_0)$
2. Probability that $n$ units are in the system $(P_n)$
3. Average number of units in the waiting line $(L_q)$
4. Average number of units in the system $(L_S)$
5. Average time a unit spends in the waiting line $(W_q)$
6. Average time a unit spends in the system  $(W_S)$
7. Probability that an arriving unit has to wait for service $(P_w)$

They are calculated depending on the model
